title: signal parsing - better input validation
body:
The helper function we use for signal name mapping does not
check for negative numbers nor invalid (too-high) ones. This
can yield unexpected error messages:
```
   # podman kill -s -1 foo
   ERRO[0000] unknown signal "18446744073709551615"
```
This PR introduces a small wrapper for it that:

  1) Strips off a leading dash, allowing '-1' or '-HUP'
     as valid inputs; and
  2) Rejects numbers <1 or >64 (SIGRTMAX)

Also adds a test suite checking signal handling as well as
ensuring that invalid signals are rejected by the command line.

Fixes: #4746

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body: I've used this as an exercise in Go namespaces; and obviously I'm still missing something because I can't figure out how to import `sigrtmax` from `github.com/docker/docker/pkg/signal/signal_linux.go`. I would prefer not to merge this PR with a hardcoded `64`. Suggestions appreciated.

Importing `sigrtmax` is impossible as it's lower-cased - Go's visibility rules are that functions and variables starting with a Capital are exported and available outside the package, lower-case ones are private and unusable.

/approve

Found a nit but otherwise LGTM

@mheon thanks for the advice. `fmt.Errorf` changed to `errors.Errorf`. Capitalization fixed, and since my only reason for capitalizing was to keep the message identical to docker's, I changed it to something I believe is more helpful anyway. The new error message required some tweaks to the test code.
I have no idea what those failures mean. Giving up for today.
